items.title: Kaspersky identifies malware framework targeting crypto investors
items.summary: Cybersecurity company Kaspersky said a newly identified malware framework is targeting cryptocurrency investors through social engineering tactics and trojanized GitHub apps.

items.title: Rust-proof your code with our new Testing Handbook chapter
items.summary: We’ve added a new chapter to our Testing Handbook: a comprehensive guide to security testing Rust programs. This chapter covers the tools and techniques we use at Trail of Bits to validate the security of Rust programs and systems. fn main() {(|f:&dyn Fn(u128)->Box< dyn Iterator<

items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: France orders country's internet service providers to block Polymarket
items.summary: The regulator cited concerns over addictive mechanics, a lack of self-exclusion tools, and a high volume of French users bypassing previous financial restrictions.

items.title: French gambling regulator orders ISPs to block Polymarket
items.summary: France’s gambling authority has ordered the country’s internet service providers to geoblock Polymarket, citing illegal gambling and market manipulation concerns.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin returns to $63,000 as Nasdaq trims large early loss
items.summary: A deepening global selloff in chipmakers dragged risk assets lower, pulling bitcoin back from the $65,000 level it reached on this week's soft inflation print.

items.title: Risk-off wave drags bitcoin below $63,000 as AI selloff spreads from stocks to crypto
items.summary: Crypto fell as AI stock fatigue and U.S.-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment, though a technical measure hints at oversold conditions heading into the weekend.

items.title: Bitcoin under $63,000 after new U.S. strike on Iran. Trump's China comment adds to uncertainty
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions and renewed fears of U.S.-China frictions are weighing on risk assets, including bitcoin.

items.title: Senator Warren requests 2026 reporting for Trump’s crypto earnings after $1.4B disclosure
items.summary: With the Senate likely voting on a crypto bill within days, Elizabeth Warren asked for information on Donald Trump’s earnings between January and July ahead of a 2027 deadline.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Inside Zcash's new node that targets Visa-scale privacy at 50,000 transactions per second
items.summary: The new Zakura client is the first live piece of a plan to take Zcash from roughly one private transaction per second to payment-network scale.

items.title: Crypto executives say digital native generations may never need a bank account
items.summary: Steakhouse Financial co-founder Adrian Cachinero says digital-native generations may rely less on banks, while Binance says younger users are already driving crypto adoption in emerging markets.

items.title: DOG Mode explains Bitcoin's next governance fight
items.summary: Leonidas' DOG Mode client challenges Bitcoin's default relay policies, reopening a philosophical debate over censorship, free markets and who really governs the network.

items.title: Trump targets Brazil's payments system while dollar stablecoins are quietly overtaking country's payments
items.summary: Washington views Brazil’s promotion of non-dollar payment channels, including Pix and the growth of stablecoins, as a potential threat to dollar-based trade, even as dollar-linked stablecoins account for about 90% of Brazil's crypto transactions.

items.title: Here is why a massive $1.6 billion in crypto liquidity is sitting idle and wasting away
items.summary: About $542 million weekly sat outside active trading ranges, meaning this capital earned zero fees and provided no market depth.

items.title: Massive bitcoin call spreads target $72,000 by month end, right when the Fed meets
items.summary: Large traders are betting on a BTC price rise to $72,000 by the end of the month, latest options market flow suggests.

items.title: Tokenization has become a strategic priority for 84% of financial firms
items.summary: A Broadridge survey found Wall Street is accelerating tokenization efforts while betting on hybrid markets where digital and traditional assets coexist.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Polymarket traders cut Clarity Act passage odds to record low as Senate delay drags on
items.summary: Polymarket bettors have cut the odds of the CLARITY Act passing this year to a record low as Senate negotiations over ethics provisions remain unresolved.

items.title: Stripe and Swift race to control the next generation of global payments infrastructure
items.summary: Crypto and blockchain experts say this week's moves show the two established finance companies are increasingly competing for control of the infrastructure behind digital payments.
